## ScreenShotPP v0.5.0

A feature release: **video recording + GIF export**. Record a screen region as a
silent MP4 with no duration limit, then trim / crop / speed it up and export to
MP4 or GIF.

### New

- **Screen recording.** A new **record shortcut** (default `Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+5`)
  opens the selection overlay in video mode; pick a region and recording starts.
  Recording is silent, unlimited in length, cursor optional, 30 fps by default,
  and crash-safe (fragmented MP4). Windows and macOS only.
- **On-screen recording HUD.** A red border marks the captured region, with a
  floating control bar showing a running timer plus **Pause / Resume** and
  **Stop** — all drawn *outside* the region, so they never appear in the video.
  Stop also works from the tray or by pressing the shortcut again.
- **Mini editor.** Trim (start/end brackets on a timeline with playhead), crop
  (draggable box), and speed (1× / 2× / 4×), with a live output-duration readout,
  precise start/end fields, and Start here / End here controls.
- **Export to MP4 or GIF.** MP4 (`+faststart`, high/small presets) or GIF
  (two-pass palette). Export GIF, then adjust trim/crop/speed and export MP4
  from the same editor without losing the source. Progress bar, explicit
  cancellation, and automatic cleanup of temporary files.
- **Separate recording delay.** The recording delay is independent from the
  screenshot delay and can be set to `0s` for instant capture.

### Notes

- Recording is a **Windows/macOS** feature; it is not available on Linux.
- The bundled ffmpeg is a static GPL build, run as a separate process;
  attribution is in the README.

### License

ScreenShotPP is open source under the MIT license.
